---
applyTo: "src/**/*.rs"
description: "Rust coding conventions for LingClaw's modular backend. Use when editing src/main.rs, src/cli.rs, src/providers.rs, src/prompts.rs, or any file under src/tools/."
---
# LingClaw Rust Conventions

## Skill + CLI Architecture

All code in this backend serves one of these roles:
- **Skill** (LLM reasoning): `build_system_prompt()` in `src/main.rs`; `prune_messages()`, `turn_len()`, `estimate_tokens()`, `estimate_tokens_for_provider()`, `context_input_budget_for_model()`, `context_input_budget_for_runtime()`, `request_message_budget_for_runtime()`, `message_budget_for_tool_defs()`, `message_token_len()`, `message_token_len_for_provider()`, `format_token_count()`, `update_session_token_usage()`, `current_daily_token_usage()`, `accumulate_daily_token_usage()`, `format_usage_block()` in `src/context.rs`; `call_llm_stream()`, `call_llm_stream_openai()`, `call_llm_stream_anthropic()`, `call_llm_simple()`, `send_with_retry()`, `convert_messages_to_openai()`, `convert_messages_to_anthropic()`, `think_level_to_reasoning_effort()`, `think_level_to_budget()`, `anthropic_prompt_caching_enabled()`, `anthropic_system_payload()`, `maybe_apply_anthropic_tool_cache_control()`, `total_anthropic_input_tokens()` in `src/providers.rs`; `TEMPLATE_FILES` (tuples with `include_str!()` embedded fallback), `templates_dir()`, `template_file_content()`, `normalize_template_text()`, `init_session_prompt_files()`, `ensure_session_workspace()`, `load_session_prompt_files_with_snapshot()`, `current_local_snapshot()`, `maybe_complete_bootstrap()`, `profile_file_has_user_edits()`, `write_bootstrap_baselines()`, `ensure_bootstrap_baselines()`, `read_bootstrap_baseline()`, `remove_bootstrap_baselines()`, `SkillMeta`, `SkillSource`, `discover_all_skills()`, `discover_skills_by_source()`, `discover_skills_in_dir()`, `parse_skill_frontmatter()`, `render_skills_catalog()`, `system_skills_dir()`, `global_skills_dir()`, `is_system_skill_disabled()`, `list_system_skill_groups()` in `src/prompts.rs`; prompt templates on disk in `docs/reference/templates/`; system skills in `docs/reference/skills/` (supports org-level nesting, e.g. `anthropics/pdf/SKILL.md`); global skills in `~/.lingclaw/skills/`; session skills in workspace `skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **Agent** (decision layer): `AgentPhase` enum (`Analyze`/`Act`/`Observe`/`Finish`), `FinishReason` enum (`Complete`/`Empty`), `AgentLoopCtx` state tracker (with `show_react`, `finish_reason`), `ToolResultEntry`, `ObservationSummary`, `summarize_observations()`, `build_observation_context_hint()` (with `consecutive_errors` for degradation hints), `maybe_annotate_observation()`, `evaluate_finish()`, `auto_think_level()` (adapts by cycle depth, observation, user message complexity, and consecutive errors), `build_finish_nudge()` (soft wrap-up nudge for deep loops), `is_simple_query()` (heuristic for fast-model routing) in `src/agent.rs`. The agent loop in `src/main.rs` uses `match react_ctx.phase()` to drive each iteration — one phase per arm. Inter-phase data flows via local variables (`pending_tool_calls`, `collected_results`, `last_observation_hint`). Phase transitions use `debug_assert!` for invariant enforcement. The Analyze arm uses `evaluate_finish()` for structured Finish vs Continue decisions, routes simple first-cycle queries to `fast_model` when configured, injects a planning nudge on cycle 0, and appends `build_finish_nudge()` for deep loops. `auto_think_level()` adapts reasoning budget based on cycle depth, observation context, user message character count, and consecutive tool errors. When `show_react` is enabled, dedicated `react_phase` WS events are emitted at each phase transition. Observation summaries are non-destructive: raw tool results are always persisted in session history untouched; summaries go to separate WS events and system prompt hints. The Finish phase spawns a non-blocking post-execution reflection that appends to daily memory.
- **CLI** (tool execution): `ToolSpec`, `ToolOutcome`, `ToolHandler`, `tool_specs()`, `tool_definitions()`, `tool_definitions_anthropic()`, `execute_tool()`, `validate_required_params()`, `validate_tool_args()`, `is_tool_error_output()`, `is_read_only_tool()` (gates parallel execution of read-only tool batches), `is_task_tool()`, `task_tool_definition_openai()`, `task_tool_definition_anthropic()` (dynamic sub-agent tool registration) in `src/tools/mod.rs`; stdio MCP discovery/execution helpers (`runtime_tool_note()`, `tool_definitions_openai()`, `tool_definitions_anthropic()`, MCP `execute_tool()`, `call_server()`) in `src/tools/mcp.rs`; `tool_*()` implementations in `src/tools/exec.rs`, `src/tools/fs.rs`, `src/tools/net.rs`; `check_dangerous_command()`, `resolve_path()`, `resolve_path_checked()` in `src/main.rs`; `check_ssrf()`, `is_private_ip()` in `src/tools/net.rs`; global usage statistics (`gather_global_today_usage()`) in `src/session_admin.rs`
- **Config** (settings layer): `Provider` enum, `JsonConfig` / `JsonSettings` / `JsonProviderConfig` / `JsonModelEntry` / `JsonMcpServerConfig` structs, `config_dir_path()`, `config_file_path()`, `load_config_file()`, `Config::load()`, `Config::resolve_model()`, `Config::available_models()`, `Config::find_model_entry()`, `Config::context_limit_for_model()`, `DEFAULT_PORT` in `src/config.rs`; `run_setup_wizard()`, `handle_cli_command()`, `handle_doctor_command()` and helpers (`parse_version_triple()`, `detect_rustc_version()`, `detect_cargo_version()`, `detect_git_version()`, `read_local_source_version()`) in `src/cli.rs`
- **Commands** (chat command dispatch): `CommandResult`, `command_result()`, `persist_session_update()`, `parse_toggle_value()`, `handle_command()` and all per-command handlers (`handle_new_command()`, `handle_model_command()`, `handle_skills_system_command()`, `show_system_skills_status()`, `toggle_system_skill()`, `skill_relative_dir()`, etc.) in `src/commands.rs`
- **Loop** (connection layer): `handle_socket()`, `run_tool_with_feedback()`, per-run cancellation (`active_runs` + `child_token()`), deferred intervention drain (`pending_interventions` injected at Analyze boundary), WebSocket plumbing in `src/main.rs`; phase-execution loop, fast-model routing for simple first-cycle queries, parallel read-only tool execution via `is_read_only_tool()` with sequential hook eval (`HookEvalResult`) before parallel dispatch, post-execution reflection (background daily memory append), planning/finish nudge injection, hook integration (BeforeToolExec/AfterToolExec in `execute_tool_call()`/`record_tool_result()`, BeforeLlmCall with re-prune in `run_analyze_phase()`, `fire_stop_command_hook()` for non-blocking /stop hooks) in `src/runtime_loop.rs` with socket helpers (OnCommand hooks for all commands including unknown) in `src/runtime_loop/socket_input.rs`; session payload delivery and command refresh in `src/socket_sync.rs`; reader/writer task setup in `src/socket_tasks.rs`; session persistence (with .tmp crash recovery on load) and migration in `src/session_store.rs`; single persistent session (`MAIN_SESSION_ID = \"main\"`), connection takeover via `connection_cancels` + `SessionRunBinding` in `active_runs`, `LiveRoundState.connection_id` ownership tracking; incremental save after every round (tool and non-tool), debounced in Observe phase (5s interval; Finish always saves); live replay for `tool_call` / `tool_progress` / `tool_result` / `task_started` / `task_progress` / `task_tool`; `SESSION_VERSION`, `migrate_session()`, `default_show_react()` for session version management
- **Hooks** (agent lifecycle): `HookRegistry`, `AgentHook` trait (with `should_run_tool`/`run_tool`, `should_run_llm`/`run_llm`, `should_run_command`/`run_command` methods), `HookInput`/`HookOutput` (incl. `ModifyToolArgs`, `ModifyToolResult`, `Reject`, `ModifyLlmParams` variants), `ToolHookInput`, `LlmHookInput`, `CommandHookInput`, `HookEvalResult` (parallel Act path), `AutoCompressContextHook`, dispatch via `run_hooks()`, `run_tool_hooks()`, `run_llm_hooks()`, `run_command_hooks()`, plus auto-compress helpers (`find_auto_compress_cutoff()`, `build_compressed_messages()`, `build_compression_source_text()`, `build_auto_summary_message()`, `build_context_compressed_event()`) in `src/hooks.rs`
- **Sub-Agents** (delegated execution): `SubAgentSpec`, `ToolPermissions`, `AgentSource`, `render_agents_catalog()`, `filter_tools_for_agent()` in `src/subagents/mod.rs`; `run_subagent()`, `SubAgentOutcome`, `build_subagent_system_prompt()`, `build_filtered_tool_defs()`, `execute_subagent_tool()` in `src/subagents/executor.rs`; `discover_all_agents()`, `find_agent()`, `parse_agent_frontmatter()`, `system_agents_dir()`, `global_agents_dir()` in `src/subagents/discovery.rs`; AGENT.md definitions in `docs/reference/agents/` (system), `~/.lingclaw/agents/` (global), `{workspace}/agents/` (session). `ToolPermissions.is_allowed()` always blocks `task` to prevent recursive delegation. Executor runs hook-integrated tools (BeforeToolExec/AfterToolExec with Reject event forwarding to parent `LiveTx`), and uses `message_budget_for_tool_defs()` for context pruning (accounts for thinking budget, tool schema tokens, and structural overhead using the sub-agent's actual filtered tool set). `execute_task_tool()` in `src/runtime_loop.rs` is the single source of `task_started`/`task_completed`/`task_failed` terminal events.
- **Memory** (structured async memory): `StructuredMemory`, `MemoryFact`, `MemoryUpdateQueue`, `load_structured_memory()`, `save_structured_memory()`, `format_memory_for_injection()` (query-aware relevance sorting), `merge_llm_response_into_memory()` (incremental update_facts/delete_facts + legacy full-replacement), `prefilter_for_memory()`, `build_conversation_excerpt()`, `memory_status()`, `memory_runtime_status()`, `memory_debug_status()` in `src/memory.rs`. Feature-gated via `config.structured_memory` (`structuredMemory` in JSON / `LINGCLAW_STRUCTURED_MEMORY` env var, default `false`). Background updater spawns at startup and processes debounced LLM extraction calls with `config.tool_timeout`-derived timeouts. Conversation excerpts filter out auto-generated compression summaries, but do include tool call names and brief tool result summaries. Merge logic uses raw `serde_json::Value` parsing to distinguish absent fields (preserve existing) from explicit null (clear). `/memory` supports summary, stats, and debug views.

When adding code, know which role it belongs to. Keep `src/main.rs` as the application loop, `src/agent.rs` for the ReAct state machine and decision layer, `src/config.rs` for configuration types and loading, `src/context.rs` for token estimation and context management, `src/hooks.rs` for agent lifecycle hooks, `src/commands.rs` for chat command handlers, `src/subagents/` for the sub-agent registry, discovery, and isolated executor, `src/session_admin.rs` for global usage statistics, `src/session_store.rs` for session persistence, `src/socket_sync.rs` for session payload delivery and command refresh, `src/socket_tasks.rs` for WebSocket reader/writer tasks, `src/providers.rs` for LLM streaming, `src/cli.rs` for CLI subcommands and setup wizard, and `src/tools/` for the tool registry and implementations.

## Patterns

- Tool functions: `async fn tool_xxx(args: &serde_json::Value, config: &Config, workspace: &Path) -> String` — always return String (result or error); workspace is per-session
- WebSocket sends: use `ws_send(&tx, &json!({...}))` for async send or `ws_try_send(&tx, &json!({...}))` for non-blocking send via mpsc channel; never raw `.send()`
- Path handling: use `resolve_path_checked(user_str, workspace)` for user-supplied tool paths so escapes are rejected with an explicit error; use `resolve_path()` only for internal sandboxed normalization where clamp-to-workspace behavior is intended. Never trust raw user paths; workspace is per-session (`~/.lingclaw/{sessionId}/workspace`)
- MCP server cwd: `mcpServers.*.cwd` is user-configured input and must also go through `resolve_path_checked()`. Do not allow MCP servers to start outside the current session workspace.
- Dangerous command check: must happen before any `tokio::process::Command`
- Error handling: return descriptive `format!("tool_name error: {e}")`, no panics
- Session ownership: LingClaw uses a single persistent session (`MAIN_SESSION_ID = "main"`). Connection takeover is handled by `connection_cancels` (cancels old WebSocket), `active_runs` with `SessionRunBinding` (cancels old agent run), and `LiveRoundState.connection_id` (guards live round cleanup). Companion `session_clients` and `live_rounds` maps track live replay/rebind state. The main session is always retained in memory (never evicted on disconnect).
- Shutdown: use `CancellationToken` (tokio-util) for cooperative cancellation; wrap long async calls in `tokio::select!` with `cancel.cancelled()`; use `.is_cancelled()` for sync checkpoints
- Per-run cancellation: each agent run creates `run_cancel = cancel.child_token()` stored in `active_runs`; use `run_cancel.cancelled()` in `select!` for LLM streaming and tool execution; `/stop` cancels via the token; clean up on run finish
- Intervention: non-command text received while the agent is busy is collected in `pending_interventions`; these are drained and injected as user messages at the next Analyze phase boundary, before the session snapshot
- SSRF: network tools must call `check_ssrf()` (async, DNS via `spawn_blocking`) before fetching; `tool_http_fetch()` builds a one-off `Client` with `redirect::Policy::none()` — never use the shared `http` client for user-controlled URLs
- UTF-8 safety: use `truncate()` (finds `is_char_boundary()` safe cut point) for all string slicing with byte limits
- Prompt flow: new sessions create all template files via `init_session_prompt_files()` and persist per-session bootstrap baselines for IDENTITY.md and USER.md. Both `init_session_prompt_files()` and `ensure_session_workspace()` also create the `skills/` directory for installable skill modules. Existing sessions reconnect via `ensure_session_workspace()`, which can restore missing core templates but must NOT recreate BOOTSTRAP.md. Prompt loading uses `load_session_prompt_files_with_snapshot()`, driven by `current_local_snapshot()`, so BOOTSTRAP mode reads `BOOTSTRAP.md + AGENTS.md` with fallback to legacy `AGENT.md`; once either IDENTITY.md or USER.md differs from that session baseline, BOOTSTRAP.md is auto-removed and normal mode reads `AGENTS.md + IDENTITY.md + USER.md + SOUL.md`, then that session's `MEMORY.md` and local today/yesterday daily memory. Skills in `skills/*/SKILL.md` are discovered by `discover_skills()` and their metadata (name + description) is injected into the system prompt via `render_skills_catalog()` each round. System skills can be disabled per-session via `disabled_system_skills: HashSet<String>` in Session; `build_system_prompt()` filters them out before rendering. `/skills-system` supports `install`/`uninstall` subcommands with pattern-based matching and parent-to-sibling expansion.
- `/new` is not session initialization. It only compresses messages, writes daily memory, and replaces history with a fresh system prompt for the current session.
- CLI install/start flow lives in `src/cli.rs`: default port is `DEFAULT_PORT` (`18989`), Linux install may use `scripts/install-linux.sh`, `systemd-install` installs `lingclaw.service`, and internal `path-install` is reserved for installer flows that need persistent PATH updates.
- Command/status UI protocol: use `progress` for in-flight command updates that must NOT clear busy state; use `success` for successful terminal command summaries that should render with success styling; use `system` for neutral command output; use `error` for failures.
- Long-running tool status protocol: preserve stable tool ids across `tool_call`, `tool_progress`, and `tool_result`. `tool_progress` should report `elapsed_ms`; `tool_result` should report final `duration_ms` for replay and UI status.
- Timeout semantics: `exec_timeout` is for shell execution in `tool_exec()`. The generic Act-phase wrapper and MCP default request budget use `tool_timeout` unless an MCP server provides `timeoutSecs`.
- Token reporting: `estimate_tokens()` is an approximate heuristic for pruning and status display, not a tokenizer-accurate count. User-visible status should label it as an estimate.
- Model config consistency: `ResolvedModel.max_tokens` should flow into both streaming and non-streaming provider calls. `Config::resolve_model()` must resolve plain model IDs deterministically, preferring the active runtime provider/config when duplicates exist.
- Provider feature gating: official API endpoints (e.g. `api.openai.com`, `api.anthropic.com`) auto-enable provider-specific features (stream usage, prompt caching); third-party compatible gateways default to disabled but can be force-enabled via config (`openai_stream_include_usage`, `anthropic_prompt_caching`) or env var (`LINGCLAW_OPENAI_STREAM_INCLUDE_USAGE`, `LINGCLAW_ANTHROPIC_PROMPT_CACHING`).
- Hook system: `HookRegistry` holds `Vec<Box<dyn AgentHook>>` dispatched at 7 lifecycle points (`BeforeAnalyze`, `AfterObserve`, `OnFinish`, `BeforeToolExec`, `AfterToolExec`, `BeforeLlmCall`, `OnCommand`). `run_hooks()` dispatches general hooks returning `Vec<serde_json::Value>` WS events. `run_tool_hooks()` dispatches tool hooks sequentially in `execute_tool_call()`/`record_tool_result()` and in the parallel Act path via `HookEvalResult`; output-type validation rejects invalid outputs (eprintln warning + NoOp fallback). `run_llm_hooks()` dispatches LLM hooks in `run_analyze_phase()` with chained `extra_system`+`think_override` propagation — maintains `running_messages` that accumulates extra_system into system message content for subsequent hooks; automatic re-prune when injection exceeds token budget. `run_command_hooks()` dispatches command hooks in `handle_idle_socket_input()` for all commands (including unknown) and via `fire_stop_command_hook()` (`tokio::spawn`) for non-blocking `/stop` hooks. `AutoCompressContextHook` fires at `BeforeAnalyze` when context exceeds input budget, compresses oldest turns, and emits `context_compressed` WS events.
- Structured memory: `MemoryUpdateQueue` receives update requests in the Finish phase (after OnFinish hooks), pre-filtered via `prefilter_for_memory()` to avoid full session clones, and processes them asynchronously with 3s debounce using a bounded queue. Memory is stored as `structured_memory.json` in the session workspace (atomic write via tmp+rename), and updater diagnostics are appended to `structured_memory.audit.jsonl` (rotation uses tmp+rename with Windows crash recovery). `build_conversation_excerpt()` filters out `## Context Summary (auto-generated)` messages to avoid feeding synthetic summaries into memory extraction, while keeping tool call names and brief tool result summaries. Merge logic parses LLM output as raw `serde_json::Value` to distinguish absent keys (preserve existing) from explicit null (clear); incremental format uses `update_facts` + `delete_facts` keys to avoid full-replacement overhead (legacy `facts`-only format still supported). HTTP client and outer timeout derive from `config.tool_timeout` (minimum 30s). `format_memory_for_injection()` injects memory into the system prompt with a 2000-char budget, with optional query-aware relevance sorting. `/memory stats` and `/memory debug` surface runtime counters and recent audit entries.
- Token usage source: Session tracks `input_token_source` / `output_token_source` (`"provider"` or `"estimated"`) to distinguish real API usage data from heuristic estimates.
- **Mandatory code review**: After completing any code change, perform a code review before committing. Check correctness, security (OWASP Top 10), style compliance, error handling, and test coverage. Run `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` as part of the review. No commit without review.
- Unit tests: all tests live in `src/tests/`, included via `#[path]` attribute at the module bottom. Keep test code separate from production code — no inline `mod tests` blocks in production `.rs` files. Pattern: `#[cfg(test)] #[path = "tests/main_tests.rs"] mod main_tests;`. Module-local `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` blocks are acceptable only in modules that already have them (e.g. `prompts.rs`, `providers.rs`, `tools/net.rs`); new tests should go under `src/tests/`.
